criteria.criterion: ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert inserts new/old topic paths via a defined normalization/canonicalization layer instead of raw, unnormalized path strings as direct B+Tree keys.
criteria.planned_check: New normalizeTopicPath helper added and called on oldPath + every newPath inside ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert before tree.Insert; verified by reading the diff and by TestSplitBtreeKeyNormalization asserting differently-formatted-but-equivalent paths (trailing slash, backslash, double slash, leading ./) resolve to the same canonical fileID via tree.Lookup(canonicalForm).

criteria.criterion: (Consistency, self-imposed) ExecuteSplitAtomic and RecoverSplitCommits must not diverge from ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert's canonicalization, since they perform the same logical operation inline.
criteria.planned_check: Same normalizeTopicPath helper reused (not reimplemented) at both additional call sites; existing TestSplitAtomicCommit (including its RecoverSplitCommits subtests) continues to pass unchanged, proving no regression for already-canonical fixture paths.

criteria.criterion: Test spec: go test ./engine/split/... -run TestSplitBtreeKeyNormalization -- insert paths with equivalent-but-differently-formatted representations (e.g. trailing separators), assert they normalize to the same canonical key.
criteria.planned_check: New TestSplitBtreeKeyNormalization function added to execute_test.go exercising exactly this scenario via ExecuteSplitBtreeInsert + tree.Lookup; run in isolation and as part of full package -race run.

regression_check: go test ./engine/split/... -race must show no new failures attributable to this change (pre-existing TestReaderDuringSplit flake, if hit, to be triaged separately per instructions)
